meter_status_voltmeter_open: Incorrect placement: circuit is nearly open
meter_guidance_ammeter_short: An ammeter has nearly zero resistance. Across parallel nodes it bypasses the load and draws extremely high current. Move it into the series path.
meter_guidance_voltmeter_open: A voltmeter has extremely high resistance. In series it nearly stops current. Connect it across the component whose voltage difference you want.
meter_guidance_ammeter_ok: The ammeter is in series, so circuit current passes through it.
meter_guidance_voltmeter_ok: The voltmeter is in parallel, so it compares electric potential across the branch while drawing negligible current.
intro_g_low: Build a circuit! Connect parts to make electricity flow and light up bulbs.
intro_g_mid: Build series and parallel circuits. Add resistors, bulbs, and switches. Watch how changing voltage affects current!
intro_g_high: Explore Ohm's Law (V=IR) by building circuits. Compare series vs parallel. Measure with ammeter and voltmeter.
intro_g_adv: Analyze DC circuits using Ohm's Law, Kirchhoff's Laws, and power dissipation. Series, parallel, and mixed configurations.
state_ready_note: Start with a load, then close the loop.
state_open_note: Close switches or add a real load.
state_current_flowing_note: Use the meters and readouts to test the model.
bench_headline: Build the loop, then prove the numbers
aria_bench_visual: Circuit bench visual showing battery, wire loop, load, and live meter readouts
svg_series_caption: Series: same current through every part
ammeter_rule_desc: Series connection; very low internal resistance.
voltmeter_rule_desc: Parallel connection; very high internal resistance.
ohm_iv_desc: For a fixed resistance, current rises in a straight line with voltage (slope = 1/R). Steeper = lower resistance.
mmc_electrons_body: The glowing dots move fast for visibility, but real electrons drift at only about 0.1 mm/s. The electric field that pushes them travels near light speed, so every bulb lights essentially the instant you connect the battery.
mmc_parallel_bold: A parallel branch LOWERS total resistance.
mmc_battery_bold: The battery sets voltage, not current.
energy_budget_title: Energy budget — where the power goes
dissipate_series_note: Biggest resistor dissipates the most (P = I²R, same I)
dissipate_parallel_note: Smallest resistor dissipates the most (P = V²/R, same V)
paradox_title: The paradox: electrons crawl, the signal races
paradox_body: The blue dots in the schematic move fast so you can see them — but real electrons barely creep. So why does the bulb light instantly? Because flipping the switch launches an electric field down the wire at nearly light speed, nudging every electron at once.
aria_paradox_svg: Two wires. In the top wire the electric field pulse races across almost instantly. In the bottom wire individual electrons drift very slowly.
kvl_desc: The sum of voltage drops around any closed loop equals the source voltage. The same current flows through every series component, so this isn't a lucky coincidence — Ohm's law forces it to balance.
circuit_open_desc: A switch is open - no current flows. Close all switches to complete the circuit.
short_circuit_detected_desc: Total resistance is below 1Ω! In real life, this could damage components or cause a fire. Add more resistance.
placeholder_ask: Ask about circuits, Ohm's Law, components...
kcl_desc: The total current entering a junction equals the total current leaving that junction.
kvl_law_desc: The sum of all voltage drops around any closed loop equals the source voltage (EMF).
aria_oscilloscope: Circuit oscilloscope visualization showing voltage, current, and capacitor charge traces
physics_resistor_how: Electrons collide with atoms in the resistive material, converting electrical energy into heat. The more collisions (higher resistance), the less current flows.
physics_resistor_analogy: Like a narrow section of pipe - it restricts water flow and creates pressure difference.
physics_bulb_how: Current heats a thin wire (filament) to ~2,500°C until it glows white-hot. The filament's resistance increases with temperature.
physics_bulb_analogy: Like rubbing your hands together fast - friction (resistance) creates heat and light.
physics_switch_how: A physical gap in the conductor. When closed, electrons flow freely. When open, the air gap has near-infinite resistance, stopping current completely.
physics_switch_analogy: Like a drawbridge - when up, nothing crosses. When down, traffic flows.
physics_led_how: A semiconductor diode that emits photons when electrons drop from a high energy band to a low one. Different materials produce different colors.
physics_led_analogy: Like a one-way door with a light - electrons can only go one direction, and they release light as they pass.
physics_ammeter_how: Measures current by detecting the magnetic field created by flowing electrons. Connected in series so all current passes through it. Has very low internal resistance.
physics_ammeter_analogy: Like a turnstile counting how many people pass per second.
physics_voltmeter_how: Measures potential difference (voltage) between two points. Connected in parallel with very high internal resistance so it doesn't affect the circuit.
physics_voltmeter_analogy: Like a pressure gauge on a water pipe - measures the push without blocking flow.
physics_capacitor_how: Two metal plates separated by an insulator. Electrons accumulate on one plate and leave the other, storing energy in an electric field. Releases energy quickly when discharged.
physics_capacitor_analogy: Like a water tank - it fills up slowly and can release all its stored water at once.
tap_component_hint: Tap a component above to learn how it works inside!
app_flashlight_desc: Battery + switch + bulb in series. Switch breaks circuit to turn off.
app_phone_charger_desc: Transformer reduces 120V to 5V. Capacitors smooth the current for steady charging.
app_car_headlights_desc: Headlights wired in parallel so if one burns out, the other stays on.
app_guitar_pedal_desc: Resistors and capacitors filter frequencies to create distortion or reverb effects.
app_traffic_light_desc: Three LED groups in parallel, controlled by a timer circuit switching between them.
app_heart_monitor_desc: Amplifies tiny electrical signals from heart muscle. Resistors set gain, capacitors filter noise.
reference_library_subtitle: Interactive references — pick a topic below to explore.
